text:
ACE Louisiana
Daily Delegate Itinerary (near locked)
March 26 – April 1, 2022 | ACE Louisiana Website
The ACE Louisiana Agenda
March 26 - April 1, 2022
New Orleans Saturday – March 26th
----------
Independent Exploration of New Orleans (OPTIONAL) 8:00 am – 1:00 pm
🦐 - Breakfast, lunch, explore the city on your own; suggestions available
----------
ACE Check-in Registration 12:30 pm – 2:00 pm
🧪 - Antigen test administered. Pick up welcome bags, badges, and PPE
📍 - New Orleans Marriott Hotel – Beureguard room, 5th Floor; (555 Canal St, New Orleans)
----------
🚍- Meet in Hotel Lobby, Prepare for Departure & Load Buses 2:00 pm – 2:30 pm
----------
City of New Orleans Tour 2:30 pm – 3:50 pm
🚍- A bus tour will be provided of New Orleans neighborhoods, cultural sites, and desired
destinations in City Park, The French Quarter, Bywater, and more. Tour ends at Chalmette
Battlefield (Battlefield Road, Chalmette, LA)
📍 - Departs New Orleans Marriott at 2:30 pm, sharp
----------
🚍- Unload Buses & Wellness Break 3:50 pm – 4:00 pm
----------
Guided Tour of Chalmette Battlefield 4:00 pm – 4:30 pm
🎙 - Led by National Parks Service. Just downriver from New Orleans in Chalmette is the site of
the January 8, 1815, Battle of New Orleans – Chalmette Battlefield. The resounding American
victory at the Battle of New Orleans soon became a symbol of a new idea: American democracy.
🌧- If raining, a 10-minute driving tour of the Battlefield and proceed to Ranch Film Studios
📍 - Chalmette Battlefield (Battlefield Road, Chalmette)
----------
🚍- Load Buses 4:30 pm – 4:40 pm
📍 - Departs the Chalmette Battlefield at 4:40 pm, sharp
----------
1

ACE Louisiana
Daily Delegate Itinerary (near locked)
March 26 – April 1, 2022 | ACE Louisiana Website
🚍- Travel: Bus from Chalmette Battlefield to the Ranch Film Studios 4:40 pm – 4:45 pm
📍 - Destination is Ranch Film Studios (8301 W Judge Perez Dr, Chalmette; enter on Packenham)
----------
🚍- Unload Buses 4:45 pm – 4:55 pm
----------
ACE Louisiana’s Delegate Icebreaker Activities 5:00 pm – 6:25 pm
🚍- A bus tour of the Ranch Film Studio’s backlot
🎙 - Welcome remarks by Meaghan McCormack, St. Bernard Economic Development Foundation
and Jason Waggenspack, Ranch Film Studio
🕴 - Activities led by Team Louisiana and the ACE Committee (the Organization of American
States (OAS), the U.S. Department of Commerce (the Economic Development Administration –
EDA), the U.S. Department of State/Permanent Mission to the OAS)
🦐 - Delegates will sample a variety of regional culinary snacks
📍 - Ranch Film Studios (8301 W Judge Perez Dr, Chalmette, LA 70043)
----------
🚍- Load Buses 6:25 pm – 6:35 pm
📍 - Departs the St. Bernard Economic Development Foundation at 5:45 pm, sharp
----------
🚍- Travel: Bus from St. Bernard EDF to the New Orleans Marriott 6:35 pm – 7:00 pm
📍 - Destination is New Orleans Marriott (555 Canal St, New Orleans)
----------
🚍- Unload Buses 7:00 pm – 7:10 pm
----------
Free Evening: Delegate Exploration in New Orleans After 7:15pm
🦐 - A perfect time to schedule informal meetings with your peers or possible strategic partners in
New Orleans. Dinner on your own. Explore and feel welcomed by all that New Orleans has to
offer from experiencing the intimacy of being in a smaller city to walking the streets of the French
Quarter; from enjoying the city’s world-class restaurants to dancing to live music on Frenchmen
Street, and more.
New Orleans Sunday – March 27th
----------
🚍- Meet in Hotel Lobby, Prepare for Departure & Load Buses 7:40 am – 8:00 am
📍 - Departs New Orleans Marriott (555 Canal St, New Orleans) at 8:00 am, sharp
----------
🚍- Travel: Bus from New Orleans Marriott to World War II Museum 8:00 am – 8:15 am
🚍- National World War II Museum, U.S. Freedom Pavilion (945 Magazine St, New Orleans);
Enter through entrance on Andrew Higgins Blvd
2

ACE Louisiana
Daily Delegate Itinerary (near locked)
March 26 – April 1, 2022 | ACE Louisiana Website
----------
Get Ready for Day One & Tour of National World War II Museum 8:15 am – 9:30 am
☕️ - Breakfast served
🗓 - Founded in 2000, it was later designated by the U.S. Congress as America's official National
WWII Museum in 2003. The museum is a Smithsonian Institution-affiliated museum.
📍 - World War II Museum, U.S. Freedom Pavilion (945 Magazine St, New Orleans)
----------
Welcome to the 14th Americas Competitiveness Exchange (ACE) 9:30 am – 10:00 am
🎙 - Welcome: Stephen Watson, President & CEO, National WWII Museum
🕴 - Participants: Led by the Organization of American States (OAS), the Government of Ecuador
as the Inter-American Competitiveness Network (RIAC) Chair Pro Tempore, the U.S. Department
of State/Permanent Mission to the OAS, and the U.S. Department of Commerce (the Economic
Development Administration – EDA)
🎙 - Special Remarks by: The Honorable Kim Osborne, Executive Secretary for  Integral
Development, Organization of American States; The Honorable Alejandra Castillo, Assistant
Secretary of Commerce for Economic Development, Economic Development Administration, U.S.
Department of Commerce; and The Honorable Carla Muirragui, Vice Minister of Production and
Industries Ministry of Production, International Trade, Investment and Fisheries - Ecuador
📍 - World War II Museum, U.S. Freedom Pavilion (945 Magazine St, New Orleans)
----------
Official Kick-Off with Southern Louisiana Leaders 10:00 am – 10:15 am
🕴 - Participants: Led by the State of Louisiana, Louisiana Economic Development (LED), the City
of New Orleans, ACE Louisiana Planning New Orleans & Co. and more
📍 - World War II Museum, U.S. Freedom Pavilion (945 Magazine St, New Orleans)
----------
Wellness Break 10:15 am – 10:45 am
🎺- Delegates will be treated to entertainment from The Victory Belles
----------
🚶‍♀️- Travel: Walk from WWII Museum to Taylor Education Center 10:15 am – 10:45 am
🚶‍♀️ - Delegates will be divided into 4 groups of 20 people and walk across the street, staggered (i.e.
10:15 / 25 / 35 / 45) to the Taylor Education Center, entering on Andrew Higgins.
📍 - Departs the World War II Museum, in groups of 20, every 10 minutes starting at 10:15 am
----------
3D Fabrication Studio Advanced Manufacturing Tour 10:15 am – 11:15 am
🎙 - Speaker: Lyle LeBlanc, Scale Innovations
🚶‍♀️ - Tour will take 15 minutes per group (20 people in each)
📍 - Taylor Education Center, 1st & 2nd Floors (612 Andrew Higgins Dr, New Orleans)
----------
🚶‍♀️- Travel: Walk to The Shops at the Contemporary Arts Center (CAC) 10:35 am – 11:30
am
3

ACE Louisiana
Daily Delegate Itinerary (near locked)
March 26 – April 1, 2022 | ACE Louisiana Website
🚶‍♀️ - When tour is complete, groups of 20 will walk across the street to the CAC, enter on Camp St,
and take the elevator to the 3rd floor.
📍 - Departs the Taylor Education Center in groups of 20, every 15 minutes starting at 10:35 am
----------
Wellness Break, Gift Shop & Tours 10:45 am – 11:25 am
🚶‍♀️ - Optional co-working space tour will take 10 minutes per group (10 people in each). There will
be a second opportunity for a tour after lunch. Plus Mr. Wolf Espresso & Books (a gift shop) on the
first floor of the Contemporary Arts Center (CAC)
📍 - The Shops at the Contemporary Arts Center (900 Camp St, 3rd Fl, New Orleans)
----------
Lunch 11:25 am – 11:45 am
🏷- Presenting Partner: Hispanic Chamber of Commerce of Louisiana (HCCL) and El Centro
🦐 - Lunch served with Honduran, Colombian & Caribbean influences
📍 - The Shops at the Contemporary Arts Center (900 Camp St, 3rd Fl, New Orleans)
----------
Latin American/Hispanic Business Ownership in New Orleans 11:45 am – 12:15 pm
🕴 - Participants: Led by Hispanic Chamber of Commerce of Louisiana (HCCL) and El Centro
🎙 - Speakers: Mayra Pineda, HCCL; Lindsey Navarro, El Centro;
📍 - The Shops at the Contemporary Arts Center (900 Camp St, 3rd Fl, New Orleans)
----------
Wellness Break & The Shops Workspace Tours 12:15 pm – 12:30 pm
🚶‍♀️ - Second opportunity to take the optional co-working space tour schedule to take 10 minutes
per group (10 people in each). This is the second opportunity for delegates to tour.
----------
Workforce Development Remarks & Panel 12:30 pm – 1:45 pm
🕴 - Participants: Led by LED’s FastStart, New Orleans Business and Economic Development for
the Greater New Orleans region (GNO, Inc.) & The City of New Orleans
🎙 - Special Remarks by: Opening remarks by The Honorable Alejandra Castillo, Assistant
Secretary of Commerce for Economic Development, Economic Development Administration, U.S.
Department of Commerce; Maria Messick, Special Advisor, Economic Development
Administration, U.S. Department of Commerce;
🎙 - Speakers: Susana Schowen, LED FastStart®; Omar Bhatti, GDIT; Josh Tatum, GNO, Inc.;
Sunae Villavaso, The City of New Orleans; Chuck Morse, ThriveNOLA
📍 - The Shops at the Contemporary Arts Center (900 Camp St, 4th Fl, New Orleans)
----------
Wellness Break 1:45 pm – 2:00 pm
⏲ - Gather at the 1st Floor of the Contemporary Arts Center
----------
🚍- Load Buses 1:50 pm – 2:10 pm
📍 - Departs the The Shops at the Contemporary Arts Center at 2:10 pm, sharp
----------
4

ACE Louisiana
Daily Delegate Itinerary (near locked)
March 26 – April 1, 2022 | ACE Louisiana Website
🚍- Travel: Bus from CAC to the New Orleans Jazz Market 2:10 pm – 2:20 pm

----------

Orleans; Raj Smoove, The Gentilly Agency; Lou Hill, Moonrise Entertainment; Gina Charbonnet,
Prospect New Orleans

----------
The Music Industry in Action & Wellness Break 3:10 pm – 3:20 pm
🏷- Presenting Partner: GNO, Inc.
🎺- Delegates will be treated to entertainment from local musicians
🍻 - Snacks and refreshments will be served
📍 - New Orleans Jazz Market (1436 Oretha Castle Haley Blvd, New Orleans)
----------
🚍- Load Buses 3:15 pm – 3:35 pm
📍 - Departs the New Orleans Jazz Market at 3:35 pm, sharp
----------
🚍- Travel: Bus from Jazz Market to New Orleans Marriott 3:35 pm – 3:45 pm
📍 - Destination is New Orleans Marriott (555 Canal St, New Orleans)
----------
🚍- Unload Buses 3:45 pm – 3:55 pm
----------
Break & Attire Change 4:45 pm – 5:00 pm
🔋 - Rest and recharge before evening festivities
----------
🚍- Meet in Hotel Lobby, Prepare for Departure & Load Buses 5:00 pm – 5:20 pm
----------
🚍- Travel: Bus from Hotel to the André Cailloux Center 5:20 pm – 5:30 pm
📍 - Destination is The André Cailloux Center for Performing Arts & Cultural Justice, formerly the
Southern Rep Theatre (2541 Bayou Rd, New Orleans) at 5:20 pm, sharp
----------
🚍- Unload Buses 5:30 pm – 5:40 pm
----------
5

ACE Louisiana
Daily Delegate Itinerary (near locked)

Equitable Development Strategies: Access to Capital 5:45 pm – 6:45 pm
🎙 - Speakers: Flozell Daniels, Jr., Foundation for Louisiana; Andy Koplin, Greater New Orleans
Foundation (GNOF); Ryan Pierce, WeFunder; Judy Reese Morse, Urban League of Louisiana;
Moderated by Shaun Randloph, Mayor's Fund of New Orleans

----------
Social Interlude & Wellness Break 6:45 pm – 7:30 pm
🏷- Presenting Partner: Baker Donelson & New Orleans Business Alliance
🦐 - Lite hors-d'oeuvres and beverages served
----------
ACE Louisiana Opening Reception: The Gateway of the Americas 7:30 pm – 8:40 pm
🎙 - Speakers: Keynote by: New Orleans Mayor LaToya Cantrell
🦐 - Heavy Hors-d'Oeuvres served after remarks
📍 - The atrium at The André Cailloux Center (2541 Bayou Rd, New Orleans)
----------
🚍- Load Buses 8:40 pm – 8:55 pm
📍 - Departs The André Cailloux Center at 8:55 pm, sharp
----------
🚍- Travel: Bus from André Cailloux Center to New Orleans Marriott 8:55 pm – 9:05 pm
📍 - Destination is New Orleans Marriott (555 Canal St, New Orleans)
----------
🚍- Unload Buses 9:05 pm – 9:15 pm
----------
New Orleans Monday – March 28th
----------
🚍- Meet in Hotel Lobby, Prepare for Departure & Load Buses 7:30 am – 7:50 am
📍 - Departs New Orleans Marriott at 7:50 am, sharp
----------
🚍- Travel: from Hotel to New Orleans BioInnovation Center (NOBIC) 7:50 am – 7:55 am
📍 - Destination is the New Orleans BioInnovation Center (1441 Canal St, New Orleans)
----------
🚍- Unload Buses 7:55 am – 8:05 am
----------
Get Ready for Day 2 & Welcome to NOBIC 8:00 am – 8:25 am
🏷- Presenting Partner: New Orleans BioInnovation Center (NOBIC)
☕️ - Breakfast served by the Cafe du Monde food truck, New Orleans’s iconic cafe known for café
au laits, chicory coffee & beignets
6
